page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.u2i-c1m2.large is an x86_64 architecture virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 2 dedicated physical cores and 2 vCPUs with a single thread per core, utilizing an Intel Xeon processor running at a clock speed of 2.7 GHz. The instance is configured with 4.0 GB of system memory, providing a resource density of 2.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or integrated GPU hardware, relying entirely on network-attached storage options. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 2 Gbps.

The dedicated CPU allocation ensures consistent processing performance without resource contention from other tenants, though the lack of hyperthreading limits parallel execution threads to two. With a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, this instance profile leans toward compute-focused workloads rather than memory-intensive applications. The absence of local storage requires external block storage attachment, which may introduce latency depending on the network configuration.

This hardware configuration is suitable for lightweight, compute-bound tasks, small-scale web serving, utility scripting, and microservices that require dedicated CPU resources but have modest memory and storage footprints.

It is equipped with 4.0 GB of RAM, providing 2.0 GB of memory per core, and operates on the KVM hypervisor. The instance does not include local storage or GPU hardware, relying on network-attached storage and offering a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps. With its dedicated CPU allocation and 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, this instance is designed for consistent performance in lightweight compute tasks, utility scripting, and small-scale web serving.
